name: minecraft-32-diamonds-fastest-test
description: Best evidence-based strategy to obtain 32 diamonds as fast as possible in Minecraft Java 1.21.11 from surface start, with prep cost excluded and no cheats/commands during execution

routes.name: Controlled Strip Mining
routes.summary: Mine at the optimal diamond Y-level using controlled tunnels/branches to maximize raw diamond throughput while minimizing air-exposure losses and risk.
routes.note: Root-level family. Broad, non-mixed mining line.
routes.decisionReason: Controlled strip mining at the diamond peak is the most deterministic high-throughput family on unknown seeds; diamond ore still peaks near Y -58/-59, and the air-exposure penalty makes controlled exposure superior to cave-facing exploration for raw output.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki diamond ore generation and air-exposure rules; search comparisons of strip/branch mining versus caving
routes.generationNote: Depth-2 child set created, evaluated, and retained for the next iteration.

routes.name: Cave Exploration
routes.summary: Sweep naturally generated caves, ravines, and aquifer walls for exposed diamond ore, prioritizing large deep cave systems and visible deposits.
routes.note: Root-level family. Broad, non-mixed cave-first line.
routes.decisionReason: Cave exploration remains a legitimate top-level family because large deepslate caves can expose many diamonds quickly and can outperform controlled mining on favorable terrain, despite lower average raw yield from air-exposure losses.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki diamond ore air-exposure rule; search results showing cave sweeping as a viable pattern and flooded caves as worth checking
routes.generationNote: Depth-2 cave-exploration subfamilies generated and evaluated; all kept for now because each is a real cave subtype with plausible seed-dependent upside.

routes.name: Structure Looting
routes.summary: Acquire diamonds directly from structure loot chests/vaults across the overworld (and relevant endgame structures), favoring high-value chests over ore mining.
routes.decisionReason: Structure looting is a real competing family on an unknown seed: buried treasure, shipwrecks, villages, mineshafts, bastions, nether fortresses, and end cities can all directly yield diamonds with meaningful chest probabilities.
routes.evidence: Search results summarizing diamond loot in buried treasure, shipwrecks, villages, mineshafts, bastions, fortresses, end cities, plus 1.21 vault loot
routes.generationNote: Depth-2 sibling families generated: overworld surface loot, underground loot, nether loot, end loot.

routes.name: Nether-Assisted Looting
routes.summary: Use Nether access and Nether-linked structures/loot routes to accelerate diamond acquisition where the Nether meaningfully contributes.
routes.note: Root-level family. Broad nether-assisted line; keep only if evidence suggests competitive average-case speed.
routes.decisionReason: Nether-assisted looting is slower to validate but still a distinct family because Nether structures can roll diamonds and may pair with progression gains; at root depth it should survive until tested against actual route timing.
routes.evidence: Search results showing nether fortress and bastion diamond loot, and the route’s potential progression acceleration
routes.generationNote: Root family kept; deeper Nether-structure and portal-timing subroutes can be generated in later depth.

routes.name: Branch Mining
routes.theme: compare subroutes within controlled mining / underground ore extraction
routes.summary: Main-tunnel plus evenly spaced side branches at the diamond band to maximize solid-block exposure per distance traveled.
routes.note: Broad controlled-mining subroute focused on geometry and spacing.
routes.decisionReason: Depth-2 keep: mechanically coherent controlled underground ore-extraction subroute; rank 1/4 among siblings.
routes.evidence: See intake/eval files for source-backed comparison.

routes.name: Flooded Aquifer Systems
routes.summary: Sweep water-filled deep cave walls and submerged edges where diamond ore is not penalized by air exposure.
routes.note: Prioritizes flooded sections, underwater walls, and visible ore on aquifer boundaries.
routes.decisionReason: Flooded aquifer cave walls most directly exploit the no-air-exposure rule while still being a broad, legitimate cave subtype; best expected cave-subfamily candidate.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki diamond ore air-exposure rule; water/lava not air; search synthesis highlighting flooded cave walls and aquifers as good exposure targets.

routes.name: Overworld Surface Loot
routes.summary: Diamond-bearing surface or near-surface structures: buried treasure, shipwrecks, desert/jungle pyramids, and village loot chains.
routes.decisionReason: Best average-case structure-looting subfamily at this depth: highest diamond density and immediate overworld accessibility; buried treasure averages 1.25 diamonds per chest in Java and can be chainable via shipwreck maps and beaches.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki / Module:LootChest buried treasure diamond table; Buried Treasure structure page; Minecraft Wiki structure loot summaries

routes.name: Large Open Caves
routes.summary: Sweep large open deepslate cave chambers and exposed walls/floors for visible diamond ore.
routes.note: Prioritizes huge open voids and high-surface-area chambers with easy traversal.
routes.decisionReason: Large open caves maximize scan speed and remain one of the main cave-exploration morphologies despite air-exposure losses.
routes.evidence: Diamond ore air-exposure rule; guide sources noting large deepslate caves can expose many diamonds quickly; open-cave mobility advantage.

routes.name: Strip Mining Variant
routes.theme: compare subroutes within controlled mining / underground ore extraction
routes.summary: Simple parallel strip/tunnel clearance at the optimal Y-level, favoring straightforward continuous excavation over side-branch geometry.
routes.note: Broad controlled-mining subroute focused on direct volume clearance.
routes.decisionReason: Depth-2 keep: mechanically coherent controlled underground ore-extraction subroute; rank 2/4 among siblings.
routes.evidence: See intake/eval files for source-backed comparison.

routes.name: Underground Loot
routes.summary: Diamond-bearing underground structures: mineshaft chest minecarts, ancient city chests, trial chambers, and stronghold loot.
routes.decisionReason: Real, testable underground structure family with mineshaft/trial-chamber/ancient-city diamond loot; weaker than overworld surface loot on average but not prune-worthy at depth 2.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki / Module:LootChest mineshaft, trial chambers, stronghold, ancient city diamond entries

routes.name: Deep Ravines and Shafts
routes.summary: Use ravines, vertical cuts, and shaft-like openings as fast access routes to deep cave networks and exposed ore.
routes.note: Treats ravines and shafts as a distinct access morphology with long exposed walls and rapid descent.
routes.decisionReason: Ravines and shafts are real cave-access morphologies with enough descent and exposure upside to remain in the tree at this depth.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki Tutorial:Diamonds; ravines and shafts provide shortcuts to the depths and can expose ores on vertical walls.

routes.name: Nether Loot
routes.summary: Diamond-bearing Nether structures: bastion remnants and nether fortress chests.
routes.decisionReason: Nether structures have direct diamond loot and can become strong on favorable seeds, but the portal/travel overhead keeps it alive rather than competitive at this depth.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki / Module:LootChest bastion remnant and nether fortress diamond entries

routes.name: Tunnel-Cave Hybrid
routes.theme: compare subroutes within controlled mining / underground ore extraction
routes.summary: Use controlled tunneling to reach and then sweep large deepslate cave volumes when they present unusually good exposure.
routes.note: Broad hybrid subroute that still stays underground and controlled.
routes.decisionReason: Depth-2 keep: mechanically coherent controlled underground ore-extraction subroute; rank 3/4 among siblings.
routes.evidence: See intake/eval files for source-backed comparison.

routes.name: End Loot
routes.summary: End-city diamond loot, the highest-upside but most delayed structure family.
routes.decisionReason: End City diamond loot is real, but the full End-access chain is too delayed for average-seed fastest-32 structure-looting compared with overworld and Nether families.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki / Module:LootChest end city diamond entry; End City access chain cost

routes.name: Lava-Front Cave Edges
routes.summary: Scan cave edges, lava pools, and lava-adjacent walls where ore generation is preserved but visibility and safety are constrained.
routes.note: Targets lava lake rims, lava tubes, and other lava-bounded cave surfaces.
routes.decisionReason: Lava-front cave edges preserve ore similarly to flooded walls and may be strong on lava-rich seeds, so it is not safe to prune at depth 2.
routes.evidence: Minecraft Wiki diamond ore generation mechanics; lava does not count as air; cave/lava-edge search guidance.

routes.name: TNT-Assisted Mining
routes.theme: compare subroutes within controlled mining / underground ore extraction
routes.summary: Use TNT to accelerate underground excavation and expose ore blocks faster than hand mining, accepting setup overhead and loss of Fortune on blasted ore.
routes.note: Derivative mechanical subroute under controlled mining.
routes.decisionReason: Depth-2 keep: mechanically coherent controlled underground ore-extraction subroute; rank 4/4 among siblings.
routes.evidence: See intake/eval files for source-backed comparison.
